comment: Single source of truth for all status enumerations used in the CE v0.1 wire format. Each binding repo (xtrape-capsule-contracts-node, etc.) generates language-native enum types from this file.

AgentStatus.description: Lifecycle state of an Agent. PENDING = registered but no heartbeat yet; ONLINE = heartbeating; OFFLINE = missed offline threshold; DISABLED = paused by admin; REVOKED = token revoked.

values.meaning: Agent registered but has not yet reported a heartbeat.
Agent has reported a heartbeat within OPSTAGE_AGENT_OFFLINE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS.
Agent has not reported within the offline threshold; sweepers transitioned PENDING/ONLINE → OFFLINE.
Admin paused the Agent; the Agent token is rejected with AGENT_DISABLED.
Admin revoked the Agent token; the Agent must re-register; token is rejected with AGENT_REVOKED.

CapsuleServiceStatus.description: Effective lifecycle state of a Capsule Service. Persisted by the backend as derived from agent.status, lastReportedAt, and admin actions.

values.meaning: Service was reported by an Agent but has not yet sent a health report.
Service has reported a healthy status within freshness threshold.
Service reports healthStatus=DEGRADED OR last health is stale but Agent online.
Service reports healthStatus=DOWN.
Last service report is older than OPSTAGE_HEALTH_STALE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS.
Owning Agent is OFFLINE/DISABLED/REVOKED — derived effective state.
Service was explicitly retired by Agent (no longer reported).

derivationOrder: If lastReportedAt older than healthStaleThreshold → STALE
Else use last reported HealthStatus mapping (UP/DEGRADED/DOWN)

HealthStatus.description: Status reported by a service via Agent health endpoint. Distinct from CapsuleServiceStatus (which is the effective derived state).

values.meaning: Service is healthy.
Service is reachable but reports a degraded condition (e.g., slow dependency).
Service self-reports unhealthy.
Service has no health probe yet (initial state).

CommandStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a Command. Strict transitions enforced by backend.

values.meaning: Command created; not yet polled by Agent.
Command was returned to the Agent in a poll response.
Agent reported startedAt.
Terminal: Agent reported successful CommandResult.
Terminal: Agent reported failure CommandResult.
Terminal: Command past expiresAt before completion.
Terminal: admin canceled the Command (reserved for EE; CE v0.1 may not expose UI but enum is reserved).

TokenStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a RegistrationToken or AgentToken.

values.meaning: Token is valid and usable.
Registration token has been consumed (one-shot).
Admin revoked the token.
Token past expiresAt.
Operation succeeded.
Operation failed; metadata.errorCode SHOULD be populated.
Operation was rejected by authorization policy.

ActorType.description: Type of actor that initiated an audited operation.

values.meaning: An admin user (browser session).
An Agent (bearer token).
A background process (sweeper, bootstrap).

TargetType.description: Type of resource targeted by an audited operation.

DangerLevel.description: Self-declared danger level of an ActionDefinition. UI uses this to choose confirmation UX.

values.meaning: Idempotent and safe (e.g. echo, runHealthCheck).
May change service state but reversible.
Destructive or hard to reverse; UI MUST require explicit confirmation.
Agent runs in-process inside a Capsule Service (CE v0.1 baseline).
Agent runs as a separate process beside services. Reserved for EE.
Agent runs as a separate fleet manager. Reserved for EE/Cloud.
